title: [v3.0.1-rhel] Bump to Buildah v1.19.10
body:
Bump Buildah to v1.19.10 in the Podman v3.0.1-rhel branch to address CVE-2022-27649 in Podman 3 from
BZ https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2125741

Signed-off-by: tomsweeneyredhat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.0.1-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
OK, I'm not really sure about the varlink drag in from go modules.....
The changes look extremely massive for one fix.
This would make a lot more sense to just patch the code in the vendor and not pull the entire vendor in.

> This would make a lot more sense to just patch the code in the vendor and not pull the entire vendor in.

We should never ever manually patch ./vendor; it's an anti pattern.  The risk of code divergence is too big and it was extremely expensive getting the 1.6.4 branch into shape.

I don't know why this PR is so big but something somewhere got bumped for unknown reasons.
They should be reverted in my opinion as it would require a newer version of golang.
@vrothberg I'm not sure what you are suggesting to revert?  If it's https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/7fec5fd30d3b, that one fixes a CVE and I'm not sure we can/should revert that....
Closing.  @flouthoc is working on bumping up to v1.19.11 and will be opening a new PR in the near future.

Apart from `varlink` all the other diff looked correct to me, since updating `github.com/prometheus/client_golang v1.11.1` explicitly in https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/7fec5fd30d3b automatically bumps `golang.org/x/sync` and `golang.org/x/sys`.
